differentials.name: Metastatic / implantation theory =  "shedding" of endometrial cells during retrograde menstruation via fallopian tubes onto peritoneum ◊  Up to 90% of women have bloody peritoneal fluid during perimenstrual period ◊  Obstructive müllerian duct anomalies are the most common cause in girls < 17 years of age
differentials.raw: Metastatic / implantation theory =  "shedding" of endometrial cells during retrograde menstruation via fallopian tubes onto peritoneum ◊  Up to 90% of women have bloody peritoneal fluid during perimenstrual period ◊  Obstructive müllerian duct anomalies are the most common cause in girls < 17 years of age

differentials.name: Dissemination theory = vascular + lymphatic spread
differentials.raw: Dissemination theory = vascular + lymphatic spread

differentials.name: Direct theory = intraoperative implantation
differentials.raw: Direct theory = intraoperative implantation (uterine surgery, amniocentesis, needle biopsy)

differentials.name: Coelomic metaplasia theory = conversion of peritoneal epithelium into functioning endometrial tissue
differentials.raw: Coelomic metaplasia theory = conversion of peritoneal epithelium into functioning endometrial tissue

differentials.name: Induction theory = combination of metastatic + metaplastic theory
differentials.raw: Induction theory = combination of metastatic + metaplastic theory

differentials.name: cyclic pelvic pain: estrogen-sensitive ectopic endometrial implants proliferate and bleed synchronously with uterine endometrium ◊  24-33% of women with pelvic pain have endometriosis: NO correlation between amount of pelvic pain + extent of disease

context: =  ENDOMETRIOSIS EXTERNA =  encysted functional endometrial epithelium + stroma in an ectopic site outside myometrium N.B.: internal endometriosis within myometrium = adenomyosis Prevalence:   5-10% of menstruating women; in 5% of post-menopausal women on estrogen replacement therapy Etiology: Mean age:   25-29 years Histo:   endometrial glands, stroma, rare smooth muscle fibers; secretory changes during 2nd half of menstrual cycle; stromal decidualization during pregnancy ◊  20-50% of infertile women have endometriosis ◊  30-50% of women with endometriosis are infertile Cause:  involvement of
